analysis: The current corpus heavily exercises the CLI/API mode selection and hook failures, but most branches in generate.py (variable overwriting), prompt.py (choice parsing), zipfile.py (URL handling), vcs.py (repo URL parsing) and config.py (path expansion) remain untouched. The grammar does not yet provide ways to trigger deep dictionary variable overwrites, boolean choice handling, URL‑based template sources, or VCS‑style repo URLs, all of which are required to hit the missing lines.
underexplored_scenario_families: template_source as a remote URL (git/hg) to hit vcs and zipfile logic
prompt handling of yes/no choices and default values
generate.py dictionary variable overwriting with nested structures
output_dir_state collisions with overwrite_if_exists / skip_if_file_exists combos
config path expansion via environment variables and user home shortcuts
reachable_by_grammar: extra_context with nested dicts to force generate.py overwrites
choice_array values that include yes/no strings
output_dir_state variations combined with json_bool flags
reachable_by_harness_only: environment variable expansion in config paths (requires harness to set env vars)
filesystem state manipulation (pre‑existing files to test skip_if_file_exists)
network fetch simulation for zipfile URL handling
unreachable_harness_limits: Cannot simulate a VCS repo without a real URL or mock; harness would need to stub network calls
Path expansion tests need external env var injection which the grammar cannot express

line_target_hints.code_signal: assignment to context[variable] when overwriting a dictionary variable
line_target_hints.required_inputs: template_object.cookiecutter_json containing a nested dict variable (e.g., "feature_flags": {"docs": true}) and extra_context_object providing a different value for the same key to trigger overwrite
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add a rule allowing extra_context_object to contain nested dictionaries and to reference existing keys, e.g., '{ "feature_flags": { "docs": false } }'

line_target_hints.code_signal: handling of list values inside a dictionary variable during overwrite
line_target_hints.required_inputs: extra_context_object with a list for a key that already exists as a list in cookiecutter_json (e.g., "license": ["MIT"]).
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Extend extra_context_object to allow list literals similar to choice_array.

line_target_hints.code_signal: conversion of user input strings to boolean via yes/no choice lists
line_target_hints.required_inputs: invocation_object.no_input = false and prompts_object containing a boolean prompt, then extra_context_object providing values like "yes", "no", "y", "n".
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Allow extra_context_object values to be raw strings (e.g., "yes", "no") instead of only quoted literals.

line_target_hints.code_signal: Path expansion and existence check for clone_to_dir when downloading a remote zipfile
line_target_hints.required_inputs: template_source set to a remote URL (e.g., "https://example.com/template.zip") and a temporary directory path supplied by the harness.
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Introduce a new alternative in template_source rule: '"https://example.com/template.zip"' (or a generic URL token).

line_target_hints.code_signal: splitting repo_url on '+' and handling git/hg prefixes
line_target_hints.required_inputs: template_source using a VCS URL like "git+https://github.com/user/repo.git".
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add a URL token rule for template_source that can include a '+' and a VCS scheme.

line_target_hints.code_signal: os.path.expandvars and expanduser on a path string
line_target_hints.required_inputs: Any path field (e.g., output_dir_state) containing environment variables like "$HOME/project" or a tilde "~/project".
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Allow path_expr to include '~' or '${HOME}' patterns.

recommended_rule_edits.rationale: Add a URL alternative to enable remote template fetching, which will exercise zipfile and vcs code paths.
Broaden to accept nested objects and list literals, allowing dictionary and list overwrites needed for generate.py branches.
Permit raw string values (e.g., "yes", "no") so prompt boolean conversion logic is triggered.
Include patterns with '~' and '${...}' to trigger config path expansion.
Add a malformed base64 string to provoke decode errors in generate.py (covers error handling branches).
